recap:
- An agentic framework requires testing three core components: tool building, choice of language model (LLM/SLM), and choice of agentic framework.
- An AI agent can analyze its environment and take actions using tools to achieve specific goals.
- Tool building is independent of the LLM/SLM and agentic framework but must be thoroughly tested for reliability.
- The chosen LLM/SLM must have the capability to decide when to call tools based on its planning, influenced by prompts and system policies.
- An LLM/SLM can be considered an agent if it can act based on the outcomes of its reasoning, despite potentially having a shallow reasoning loop.
- The agentic framework orchestrates the model's behavior through policies related to state, memory management, and tracing.
- The demonstration will focus on configuring a LangChain agent with Unity Catalog tools, executing it, and performing basic tracing with MLflow.
